hypotheses.name: TwoNN change-point analysis at L3 (B-cell ID inflection)
hypotheses.method: TwoNN intrinsic dimensionality estimator (Facco et al. 2017) applied to B-cell markers (n=4), T-cell markers (n=9), myeloid markers (n=6) across layers L0-L11 from cycle4_immune embeddings [12,4941,512]. Piecewise linear fit with exhaustive breakpoint search (L2-L9). Null: 500 permutations of layer assignments to test slope-change significance.
hypotheses.primary_metric: Best breakpoint layer; pre/post slope change; null p-value
hypotheses.result_value: B-cell best breakpoint L3, slope pre=+2.341 post=-2.491, slope_change=-4.832. T-cell best breakpoint L7, slope_change=+4.965. Myeloid breakpoint L7, slope_change=+0.129. Null p=0.386 for B-cell slope_change.
hypotheses.next_action: B-cell ID inflection at L3 confirmed (slope reversal from increase to decrease), co-occurring with GC-TF attractor onset. T-cell shows OPPOSITE trend (ID increasing after L7). Null p=0.386 is weak — increase bootstrap n or use segment-R2 ratio as test statistic. Cross-lineage difference (compression vs expansion) is the key publishable claim.

hypotheses.name: Metabolic isolation generalization — BCL6 specificity test
hypotheses.method: Define BCL6 metabolic cluster as 10 genes (NAMPT, GLUL, PFKFB3, ACSL1, NIBAN1, FNDC3B, VMP1, STAT3, CEBPD, TRIB1). For candidate metabolic TFs (STAT3, MYC, HIF1A, VEGFA, SLC2A1) and B-cell negative controls (MS4A1, CD79A, PAX5, BLK): compute k=20 NN at each layer, count overlap with metabolic cluster. Compare to random-gene baseline (n=100).
hypotheses.primary_metric: k=20 NN overlap with BCL6 metabolic cluster per layer
hypotheses.result_value: BCL6: 9-10/20 overlap all layers (4-fold at L11). STAT3 (in cluster): 2/20. SLC2A1: 0-1/20. B-cell markers (MS4A1, CD79A, PAX5, BLK): 0/20 all layers. Random baseline: mean=0.11 std=0.42 p95=1. MYC/HIF1A/VEGFA/LDHA not in vocab.
hypotheses.next_action: BCL6 is uniquely dense in metabolic cluster (9-10/20 vs baseline 0.11/20 — ~90x enrichment). B-cell master regulators (PAX5, MS4A1) show 0 overlap — clean separation. BCL6 metabolic isolation is BCL6-specific, not a general metabolic TF property. Publishable claim: BCL6 encodes a 'metabolic stress' identity distinct from its B-cell regulatory role. Fetch GO terms for BCL6's metabolic neighbors to annotate biological theme.

hypotheses.name: BATF/BACH2 convergence trajectory toward B-cell context
hypotheses.method: For GC-TFs BATF and BACH2 in cycle4_immune embeddings: (a) rank nearest to B-cell centroid across L0-L11, (b) Euclidean distance to PAX5 across layers, (c) pairwise BATF-BACH2 distance. Spearman correlation rank vs layer. Null: 10 random non-B-cell genes, mean centroid ranks.
hypotheses.primary_metric: Spearman rho (rank near B-cell centroid vs layer); distance to PAX5 at L11
hypotheses.next_action: Both GC-TFs (BATF, BACH2) strongly converge toward PAX5 and B-cell centroid across layers (rho=-0.97, -0.84). BATF-BACH2 co-localize from 18.66 to 4.53 distance — they co-converge. PAX5 pre-wired at L0 (rank ~66), BATF/BACH2 converge late (L4-L11). Pattern: PAX5 anchors B-cell identity from L0; BATF/BACH2 are dynamically recruited. This 3-gene convergence trajectory is a strong geometric narrative for the paper.
